vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix logits processor when prompt_logprobs is not None
vllm.author: huyiwen
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This is a resubmission of #3023.
This handles prompt_logprobs by skipping rows in logits added for the prompt tokens (prompt logprobs are not processed)
Comparing to the previous one, this pr resolves the comment of @tjohnson31415.
Related issue: #2141
Related PR: #2800
FIX #2800

vllm.title: [Bugfix] handle prompt_logprobs in _apply_min_tokens_penalty
vllm.author: tjohnson31415
vllm.bodyExcerpt: I implemented _apply_min_tokens_penalty in #3124, but was not aware that having prompt_logprobs in the sampling parameters affects the shape of logits. This PR fixes a bug that would occur if using min_tokens and prompt_logprobs together where the start_idx cursor would not be tracking across sequen

vllm.title: [Model][AMD] ROCm support for 256 head dims for Gemma
vllm.author: jamestwhedbee
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Thanks to @jpvillam-amd's contributions on #3643, Gemma should have ROCm support
In my testing, I had to make these additional but trivial tweaks to actually use google/gemma-2b-it
This was tested on an MI100
FIX #3073 (link existing issues this PR will resolve)

vllm.title: [Doc] Add doc to state our model support policy
vllm.author: youkaichao

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Remove key sorting for `guided_json` parameter in OpenAi compatible Server
vllm.author: dmarasco
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
PR #2819 added guided decoding, however the behavior is inconsistent/unexpected. Calling the API with a stringified JSON adheres to the keys in the order provided in the JSON Schema; However, providing a JSON (e.g. a dict via the openai client) sorts the keys.
BEFORE SUBMITTING, PLEASE READ THE CHEC

vllm.title: [Doc] Fix getting stared to use publicly available model
vllm.author: fpaupier
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Previous model meta/llama-2 required special access to the model card on hugging face leading to an exception when starting the reference example.
The access error with meta-llama/Llama-2-7b-hf  was:
OSError: You are trying to access a gated repo.
Make sure to have access to it at https://huggingfac

vllm.title: [Bugfix] handle hf_config with architectures == None
vllm.author: tjohnson31415
vllm.bodyExcerpt: hf_config is of type PretrainedConfig which has the field architectures that is an optional list. When not specified the attribute is given the value None (REF). So getattr() returns None which then causes a TypeError: argument of type 'NoneType' is not iterable error due to the in statement. This P

vllm.title: [WIP][Core][Refactor] move vllm/model_executor/parallel_utils into vllm/distributed and vllm/device_communicators
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
I don't place vllm/device_communicators under vllm/distributed/device_communicators, because device communicators can also be used in a single process.
Main idea of the change:
vllm/model_executor/parallel_utils/parallel_state.py and vllm/model_executor/parallel_utils/communication_op.py --> vllm/di

vllm.title: [Core][5/N] Fully working chunked prefill e2e
vllm.author: rkooo567
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR is a part of the RFC #3130.
This PR enables chunked prefill e2e. Note that chunked prefill is an experimental feature now (though it is actively used within Anyscale), and I will start serious benchmark with this PR.
The feature can be enabled by using enable_chunked_prefill. The chunking is

vllm.title: [Core][Model] Use torch.compile to accelerate layernorm in commandr
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
If we just add @torch.compile to forward function, pytorch will try to trace self, and everytime self changes, it tries to re-compile.
However, self actually does not matter in our computation. We just need a standalone layernorm function. So we can extract the computation in a standalone way, so th

vllm.title: [Test] Add xformer and flash attn tests
vllm.author: rkooo567
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Currently, we only test flash attn implementation in the master. We should make sure to test xformer attention works e2e.
Ideally, we should run model-only test, but there's no way to do this now, so I instead added e2e tests with basic_correctness.

Refactor ops and cache_ops layer. Add an abstraction ops layer and will use vllm._C.ops by default.
